notes: Primary source is Cisco's own community knowledge base article. IEC C14 inlet inferred from third-party replacement power cords being listed as C13-to-NEMA-5-15P (i.e., device side is C14); this is consistent with standard practice for 1U rack switches of this class. USB port listed as USB-A Type-A based on Cisco 350 series documentation describing it as a standard USB slot for storage; USB 2.0 grade inferred (not explicitly stated in available sources). Console port is RJ45 confirmed by Cisco community article. The 350 series originally shipped with both RJ45 and mini-USB console ports; per a separate Cisco article, the mini-USB console port was later removed — the SG350-28P (non-CBS variant) retains the RJ45 console. Cisco.com product pages returned HTTP 403 and could not be accessed directly.

ports.name: Gigabit PoE+ 1–24
ports.note: 24 x 10/100/1000 PoE+ ports (802.3af/at/bt 60W); 195W total PoE budget

ports.name: Combo 25
ports.note: Shared with SFP slot 25; RJ45 and SFP are mutually exclusive

ports.name: Combo 25 (SFP)
ports.note: Shared combo port with RJ45 25; only one active at a time

ports.name: Combo 26
ports.note: Shared with SFP slot 26; RJ45 and SFP are mutually exclusive

ports.name: Combo 26 (SFP)
ports.note: Shared combo port with RJ45 26; only one active at a time

ports.name: Console
ports.note: RJ45 serial console port, 115200 bps default; located on back panel

ports.name: USB
ports.note: USB Type-A for file management (config/firmware/SYSLOG backup); FAT32 read/write, NTFS read-only

ports.name: AC In
ports.note: IEC C14 inlet; 100–240V AC, 50/60 Hz universal; max 263W with full PoE load at 110V; power cord (C13 to NEMA 5-15P) included
